action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 10's checkpoint achieved test AP 0.2087 (new best) with a 512-dim 8-layer MPNN, virtual node, and mean+max pooling. Validation AP peaked at 0.2303. Training loss was still decreasing, suggesting capacity and/or regularization limits. Analysis: To push past 0.21 AP, I need a more expressive architecture and better handling of extreme class imbalance. Key improvements: (1) Scale width to 768 to increase capacity; (2) Add Jumping Knowledge (JK) connections that concatenate all layer outputs and project back, capturing multi-scale structural information; (3) Switch aggregation from mean to sum (more expressive, LayerNorm stabilizes); (4) Introduce edge dropout (0.1) as graph augmentation; (5) Replace BCE with FocalLoss (gamma=2, alpha=0.75) to focus on hard positives given 1.4% prevalence; (6) Maintain an EMA of weights (decay=0.999) and validate/save the EMA model, which often generalizes better; (7) Increase dropout to 0.4 and weight decay to 3e-5 to combat the val-test gap; (8) Train for 50 epochs with 5-epoch warmup + cosine LR. Estimated time ~80-90 min. With 4.4 hours left, this is affordable. Decision: Update model.py and train.py with the above, keep predict.py compatible, and start the 50-epoch training run.
